name: Luintra – tour around Monasterio San Pedro de Rocas – Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve
name_original: Luintra – vuelta por Monasterio San Pedro de Rocas – Reserva de Biosfera Ribeira Sacra
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Nogueira de Ramuín are characterized by the dramatic landscapes of the Ribeira Sacra region in Galicia, Spain. The area features impressive Sil Canyons carved by the Sil River and the confluence of the Miño and Sil rivers, creating a unique geography. Cyclists encounter steep slopes with terraced vineyards, known for heroic viticulture, alongside lush forests of oak, birch, and chestnut. Numerous panoramic viewpoints offer expansive vistas of the rivers and canyons. Best touring cycling routes around Nogueira De Ramuín The most popular touring cycling route is Luintra – tour around Monasterio San Pedro de Rocas – Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve, a 15.9 miles (25.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This route explores the Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve and passes by the historic Monasterio San Pedro de Rocas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cachamuíña Reservoir – Cachamuíña Reservoir loop from Boeiros, a moderate 15.5 miles (25.0 km) path. This route offers views of the Cachamuíña Reservoir and the surrounding Galician countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Caserío de Fontes – Sil River Canyon loop from Os Peares, a 70.0 miles (112.7 km) trail leading through the impressive Sil River Canyon, often completed in about 8 hours 38 minutes. Touring cycling around Nogueira de Ramuín is defined by dramatic river canyons, ancient forests, and terraced vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging excursions. The routes in Nogueira de Ramuín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nogueira de Ramuín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: There are over 150 touring cycling routes in Nogueira De Ramuín, offering a wide range of options for cyclists of all abilities. These routes explore the dramatic landscapes of the Ribeira Sacra region, including the impressive Sil Canyons and ancient forests.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: The routes in Nogueira De Ramuín cater to various skill levels. You'll find 5 easy routes, 24 moderate routes, and 130 difficult routes. The terrain often includes steep slopes due to the region's unique geography, with options ranging from gentle forest tracks to more demanding excursions with significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: The touring cycling routes in Nogueira De Ramuín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the stunning natural beauty, the challenging climbs, and the historical landmarks along the way.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural landmarks to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Nogueira De Ramuín is rich in history and culture. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/a&gt;, a significant monastic center. Other notable sites include well-preserved Romanesque churches from the 12th century, such as the church of San Martiño de Nogueira, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of San Martín de Ourense&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its 'heroic viticulture' on terraced vineyards, adding a unique cultural element to the landscape.

Q: Can I cycle past any natural wonders or viewpoints in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its breathtaking natural beauty. Many routes offer views of the impressive Sil Canyons, carved by the Sil River, and the confluence of the Miño and Sil rivers. You can also find numerous panoramic viewpoints, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Boqueiriño Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra Longa Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering spectacular vistas of the rivers and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by touring bike in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: Yes, you can include the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5016863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in your touring cycling adventure. It's a notable natural monument in the area, offering a refreshing stop amidst your ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Nogueira De Ramuín offers several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachamuíña Reservoir – Cachamuíña Reservoir loop from Boeiros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 25 km path that provides scenic views of the reservoir and the Galician countryside. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Boqueiriño Viewpoint – Amandi loop from Miradoiro do Boqueiriño&lt;/a&gt;, a more challenging 50 km route.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: The Ribeira Sacra region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from spring through autumn. Spring brings lush greenery and comfortable temperatures, while autumn showcases the vibrant colors of the vineyards. Summer can be warm, especially in the canyons, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred. Winter cycling is possible but can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caserío de Fontes – Sil River Canyon loop from Os Peares&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 112.7 km trail. This route leads through the impressive Sil River Canyon and typically takes over 8 hours to complete, offering an immersive experience of the region's dramatic landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: While many routes in Nogueira De Ramuín feature significant elevation changes, there are easier options suitable for families. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' or 'moderate' that stick to flatter sections or well-maintained forest tracks. Always check the route details for elevation and terrain before heading out with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling trails in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: Many outdoor areas in Nogueira De Ramuín are dog-friendly, but it's always best to check specific trail regulations. When cycling with your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, kept on a leash where required, and that you carry enough water for both of you, especially on longer routes or during warmer weather.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Nogueira De Ramuín?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific starting point of your chosen route. Popular trailheads and attractions like the Monastery of Santo Estevo de Ribas de Sil or the Embarcadero de Santo Estevo often have designated parking areas. For more remote routes, look for small village parking or roadside pull-offs, ensuring you do not obstruct local traffic or access.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists in Nogueira De Ramuín or the wider Ribeira Sacra region?
A: Public transport options in the rural Ribeira Sacra region can be limited. While some bus services connect larger towns, carrying bicycles might be restricted or require prior arrangement. Catamaran tours on the Sil Canyons are popular for sightseeing, but they are not typically designed for transporting bicycles for touring. It's advisable to plan your routes with starting and ending points accessible by car if you're bringing your own bike.
tips: The path of the Loña river. You can follow a section of the main road along the river if you have time and see the old water mills along the river.

Excavated in the natural rock of the mountains near the town of Esgos in Ourense is the abandoned monastery of San Pedro de Rocas. This ancient cult building is considered one of the oldest in Galicia and the only one that still retains part of its original structure.

waypoints.name: Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas
